mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, the equity discount rate declines and valuation multiples expand, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods with falling FEDFUNDS and stable CPIAUCSL, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPIAUCSL above the recent inflation trend forces real-rate expectations higher and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect is immediate when inflation surprises are not offset by lower policy rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 3-month average by more than 0.3 index points
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-crossing events are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign of the effect flips after including FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE reading reflects tight labor markets and sustained household income support, which stabilizes earnings expectations and supports S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment is rising quickly from a low base.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0% and PAYEMS non-declining over the last 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment regimes do not outperform baseline over 60 days, or if returns are negative despite stable PAYEMS in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS signals broad employment growth and stronger consumer spending capacity, which lifts cyclical earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism breaks when payroll gains are accompanied by rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll-growth triggers do not produce positive 20-day returns on average, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates, realized consumer demand is improving and equity revenue expectations rise, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest if GDP growth is not simultaneously slowing.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales acceleration does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal vanishes when quarterly GDPC1 is weakening.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and supply-side activity, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal weakens if housing starts are simultaneously contracting sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day returns do not follow sustained INDPRO improvement, or if the effect is absent whenever HOUST is falling on a 2-month basis.

mechanism_narrative: A deterioration in HOUST signals weakening construction demand and a softer real economy, which reduces cyclical earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is most reliable when broader labor conditions are also softening.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% versus the prior 2-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered instances, or if the signal disappears when UNRATE is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly GDPC1 growth trend indicates broad aggregate demand expansion, which should support corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect fails when GDP growth is decelerating while inflation is still elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 trend does not predict positive 60-day SPX returns above baseline, or if the relationship reverses during periods of slowing GDP and sticky CPIAUCSL.
